Task #11 (Navigation + Dashboard) is committed and complete (f71047e). Moving to Task #15: Implementation Step 2 — Inbox Capture (US-GTD-1). Luca remains Driver. Please review his work as it progresses and send feedback from your area of expertise.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good, Step 1 is committed. Let me send proactive HTMX guidance to Luca for US-GTD-1 (Inbox Capture), since this is where the quick capture form needs real HTMX attributes.

   360→
   361→**As a** GTD practitioner,
   362→**I want to** quickly capture anything on my mind into my inbox,
   363→**So that** I can get it out of my head and process it later.
   364→
   365→#### Acceptance Criteria
   366→
   367→- [ ] Inbox capture input is always accessible (prominent on the main page, and available
   368→  via a persistent capture input on every authenticated page)
   369→- [ ] Capture is fast: type and press Enter, item appears in inbox
   370→- [ ] No categorization required at capture time (that happens during clarify)
   371→- [ ] Inbox items show in a list ordered by capture time (oldest first -- process in
   372→  FIFO order, like a physical inbox)
   373→- [ ] Inbox count is visible in the navigation so users know how many items need processing
   374→- [ ] Input has a visible `<label>` and helpful placeholder text
   375→- [ ] After capturing, the input field is cleared and focus returns to it
   376→- [ ] Empty or whitespace-only submissions are silently ignored
   377→- [ ] Title has a maximum length of 300 characters
   378→- [ ] Works without JavaScript (full page reload); HTMX enhances
   379→- [ ] `aria-live` region announces "Captured to inbox" after HTMX swap
   380→- [ ] Capture event is logged with structured tracing
   381→
   382→#### Accessibility
   383→- [ ] All interactive elements are fully keyboard operable
   384→- [ ] Capture input has a visible `<label>` associated via `for`/`id`
   385→- [ ] Focus management: after capture, focus returns to the input
   386→- [ ] Skip link bypasses header capture input to reach main content
   387→- [ ] Screen reader announces inbox count change after capture
   388→
   389→#### Empty State
   390→- When the inbox is empty, show: "Inbox zero -- nothing to process. Capture something
   391→  new or start your weekly review." with links to capture input and weekly review.
   392→
   393→#### Edge Cases
   394→- Adding with only whitespace (silently ignored)
   395→- Rapid successive captures
   396→- Very long text (at or beyond max length)
   397→- Special characters, HTML, emoji
   398→
   399→#### Success Metric
   400→Capturing a thought takes under 3 seconds from thought to inbox.

file.content:
### US-GTD-1: Capture to Inbox

**As a** GTD practitioner,
**I want to** quickly capture anything on my mind into my inbox,
**So that** I can get it out of my head and process it later.

#### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Inbox capture input is always accessible (prominent on the main page, and available
  via a persistent capture input on every authenticated page)
- [ ] Capture is fast: type and press Enter, item appears in inbox
- [ ] No categorization required at capture time (that happens during clarify)
- [ ] Inbox items show in a list ordered by capture time (oldest first -- process in
  FIFO order, like a physical inbox)
- [ ] Inbox count is visible in the navigation so users know how many items need processing
- [ ] Input has a visible `<label>` and helpful placeholder text
- [ ] After capturing, the input field is cleared and focus returns to it
- [ ] Empty or whitespace-only submissions are silently ignored
- [ ] Title has a maximum length of 300 characters
- [ ] Works without JavaScript (full page reload); HTMX enhances
- [ ] `aria-live` region announces "Captured to inbox" after HTMX swap
- [ ] Capture event is logged with structured tracing

#### Accessibility
- [ ] All interactive elements are fully keyboard operable
- [ ] Capture input has a visible `<label>` associated via `for`/`id`
- [ ] Focus management: after capture, focus returns to the input
- [ ] Skip link bypasses header capture input to reach main content
- [ ] Screen reader announces inbox count change after capture

#### Empty State
- When the inbox is empty, show: "Inbox zero -- nothing to process. Capture something
  new or start your weekly review." with links to capture input and weekly review.

#### Edge Cases
- Adding with only whitespace (silently ignored)
- Rapid successive captures
- Very long text (at or beyond max length)
- Special characters, HTML, emoji

#### Success Metric
Capturing a thought takes under 3 seconds from thought to inbox.

input.content:
Luca, Carson here. HTMX guidance for US-GTD-1 (Inbox Capture). This step has two distinct HTMX interactions to get right: ## 1. The Inbox Page Capture Form On the `/inbox` page itself, the capture form adds items to the inbox list below it. This is the same pattern as our existing todo add form in `todos.html`: ```html
``` The explicit `hx-post` and `hx-target` override the inherited `hx-boost` from ``. The response appends the new item to the list. The form resets on success. Standard pattern — we've done this before. The server handler checks `HX-Request`: - **HTMX request**: Return just the inbox item HTML fragment (the new `
  • `) - **Non-HTMX request**: Redirect to `/inbox` (full page reload shows the updated list) Include an `HX-Trigger` response header with the `announce` event: `{"announce":"Captured to inbox"}` so the `aria-live` region announces it. ## 2. The Header Quick Capture Form (on every other page) The quick capture in `gtd_nav.html` is trickier because the inbox list isn't on the page. The form needs to: 1. POST to `/inbox` 2. Show a brief confirmation ("Captured!") 3. Clear the input 4. Update the inbox count in the nav Here's the pattern: ```html
    ``` The server handler needs to distinguish between these two contexts. When the request comes from the header capture (not on the inbox page), the response should be a small confirmation fragment like `Captured!` instead of an inbox list item. **How to distinguish**: The simplest approach is to check if a specific HTMX header tells you which target is being used. The `HX-Target` request header tells the server what element ID the response will be swapped into. If `HX-Target` is `capture-confirmation`, return the confirmation fragment. If it's the inbox list class/ID, return the list item fragment. If no `HX-Request` header at all, do the standard redirect. **Updating the inbox count in the nav**: Use an out-of-band swap. The response includes an `hx-swap-oob="true"` element that updates the count: ```html Captured! (4) ``` This means the nav needs a `` around the count number so the OOB swap has a target. Update `gtd_nav.html` to wrap the inbox count. ## 3. Avoiding Duplicate Capture Inputs on the Inbox Page US-GTD-8 says: "on the Inbox page, the header capture input and the inbox page's own capture input are the same form." The simplest approach: on the inbox page template, don't render the page-level capture form. The header capture form IS the capture mechanism. On the inbox page, make the header form target the inbox list instead of the confirmation span. Actually, the cleanest implementation: on the inbox page, use a `{% block %}` override to suppress the header capture form, and show only the page-level form that targets the list. This avoids the complexity of one form needing two different targets depending on the page. ## 4. FIFO Ordering The acceptance criteria say "oldest first" (FIFO). This means `hx-swap="beforeend"` is correct — new items append to the bottom of the list. The oldest items are at the top, newest at the bottom. Users process from top to bottom, like a physical inbox stack. Wait — actually, a physical inbox has newest on top. But FIFO processing means you process the oldest first, so oldest at the top of the list is correct for a digital interface. `hx-swap="beforeend"` appends new items at the bottom. Correct. Let me know if you have questions. The key decisions are: 1. How to distinguish header capture vs inbox page capture (I recommend `HX-Target` header check) 2. How to handle the dual-form problem on the inbox page (I recommend suppressing one via template blocks)
input.summary: HTMX guidance for inbox capture: two form contexts, OOB count, FIFO
